summary: All 19 taint flows flagged by static analysis are false positives. The plugin uses proper escaping (esc_url, esc_attr, selected()), hardcoded output values, admin-only contexts, or input sanitization (preg_replace, strip_tags, sanitize_text_field) that prevent exploitation. Two instances of missing output escaping were found (WPProfileFieldParserTrait.php:150 and FieldsShortcodeCallback.php:1098) but the stored filenames are sanitized to [A-Z0-9._-] at upload time by FileUploader::process(), making XSS injection infeasible through normal plugin flows.

verdicts.reasoning: The billing_country value from get_user_meta() is used only as a lookup key for ppress_array_of_world_states(). It never reaches HTML output directly. Field keys and types come from hardcoded arrays in CheckoutFields::standard_billing_fields(). The shortcode [edit-profile-cpf] handles its own rendering and escaping.

Widget instance text values are sanitized with strip_tags() in the update() method, which removes all HTML tags. Additionally, widget configuration is admin-only (WordPress widgets are managed by users with manage_options capability). Admin-to-admin stored XSS is a won't-fix by WordPress design.

$_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] is escaped with esc_url_raw() which strips dangerous protocols (javascript:, data:). While esc_url() would be more correct for HTML output context, esc_url_raw still prevents protocol-based XSS. Practical risk is negligible.
$_SERVER['REQUEST_URI'] is properly escaped with esc_url(), the correct escaping function for URLs in HTML context. This strips dangerous protocols and applies kses-style filtering.

Missing output escaping: $filename from pp_uploaded_files user meta is echoed directly into HTML. However, FileUploader::process() sanitizes filenames with preg_replace('/[^A-Z0-9._-]/i', '_', $filename) at line 103 of FileUploader.php, which replaces all HTML-special characters (<, >, ', ", &) with underscores. All code paths storing pp_uploaded_files go through FileUploader::init(). No practical XSS exploit path exists. Code quality issue only.

The pp_profile_avatar user meta is set exclusively by ImageUploader::process(), which sanitizes filenames with preg_replace to [A-Z0-9._-] then MD5-hashes the base name. Path traversal characters (../) cannot survive this pipeline. User can only act on their own avatar via get_current_user_id().
Same as path-001: pp_profile_cover_image is set via ImageUploader::process() with MD5-hashed, sanitized filenames. No path traversal possible through the stored slug.

Source is get_option() (database value, not direct user input). Sink uses esc_url() for escaping. Data is sanitized with sanitize_text_field() before storage. Page is admin-only (manage_options capability required). Admin self-XSS via stored option is not exploitable.

$_GET['form-type'] is only used in a boolean comparison against a constant (FR::LOGIN_TYPE). The ternary outputs either the hardcoded string 'pp-type-active' or null. No user-controlled data reaches the echo statement. Admin-only page.
Same pattern as xss-007. Ternary outputs 'pp-type-active' or null based on comparison with FR::REGISTRATION_TYPE. No user data in output.
Same pattern as xss-007. Ternary outputs 'pp-type-active' or null based on comparison with FR::PASSWORD_RESET_TYPE. No user data in output.
Same pattern as xss-007. Ternary outputs 'pp-type-active' or null. No user data in output.
Same pattern as xss-007. Ternary outputs 'pp-type-active' or null. No user data in output.
Same pattern as xss-007. Ternary outputs 'pp-type-active' or null. No user data in output.

Source is $_GET['preset'] with sanitize_text_field applied. Sink is WordPress selected() function which only compares values and outputs 'selected="selected"' or empty string. User-supplied value is never directly echoed into HTML. Admin-only page.

Source is $_GET['plan'] with sanitize_text_field applied. Sink is WordPress selected() function which only does comparison, outputting 'selected="selected"' or empty string. Option values and text come from database (plan->id, plan->name). Admin-only page.

The $type parameter is allowlisted to exactly ['social-login', 'debug'] in ToolsSettingsPage.php:27 via in_array() check before being passed to ppress_clear_error_log(). The $file_token comes from get_option() (not user input). Requires manage_options capability and nonce verification.

Source is get_user_meta() returning a stored attachment ID (integer), echoed into a hidden input value attribute. While technically missing esc_attr(), the value is an attachment ID set by the plugin via update_user_meta and is always an integer. Admin-only page (user profile editing). Requires separate vulnerability or DB access to exploit.

Source is site_url() -- a WordPress core function returning the site URL from wp_options. This is an admin-configured setting, not user-controlled input. An attacker cannot control site_url() without already having admin access. File also has ABSPATH check preventing direct access.
